title: Fix maintenance mode toggle and registered users loading in admin panel

requirements.text: Fix the maintenance mode toggle in UsersControlsPanel so that calling toggleMaintenanceMode via the admin actor succeeds without throwing an error. Ensure the admin actor is correctly initialized with admin credentials before the toggle call is made, and that the backend toggleMaintenanceMode function authorizes the admin caller properly.

quotes: Failed to update maintenance mode. Please try again.

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the maintenance mode toggle in the admin panel does not show 'Failed to update maintenance mode' error
The toggle correctly switches between 'App is Live' and 'Maintenance Mode' states
The backend toggleMaintenanceMode function accepts the admin actor call without rejection
The updated maintenance mode state persists and is reflected on next load

requirements.text: Fix the registered users list in UsersControlsPanel so that getAllUsers (or equivalent) is called via a correctly initialized admin actor with proper permissions, and the list of registered users is successfully fetched and displayed. Ensure the query does not fail with an authorization or initialization error.

quotes: Failed to load users. Please try again.

acceptanceCriteria: The Registered Users section displays the list of registered users without showing 'Failed to load users' error
Users can be searched by Unique ID or name
The backend user listing function authorizes the admin actor call and returns all users
Block/unblock actions remain functional after the fix

constraints: Do not change immutable frontend paths (useInternetIdentity, useActor, main.tsx, ui components)
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor
nonGoals: Adding new admin features beyond fixing the two broken ones
Changing authentication flow for regular users
